description_long: This object consists of three open-ended wrenches of varying sizes, arranged upright and closely grouped together. The wrenches are made of metal, with two showing significant darkening and surface wear, while the third appears lighter and less corroded. Each wrench features a U-shaped open jaw at both ends, with the larger wrench displaying visible size markings and manufacturer text along its shaft. The surfaces of the wrenches are mostly smooth but show signs of use, including scratches, discoloration, and patches of rust, especially on the darker wrenches. The overall form is utilitarian, with no visible rubber grips or ergonomic enhancements. These wrenches are typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance settings.
description: A set of three metal open-ended wrenches, two dark and worn, one lighter, grouped upright with visible size markings and signs of use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open jaws of all three wrenches, with size markings and wear most visible on the left and right wrenches.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wrenches and the lighter color of the central wrench, with open jaws facing downward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the wrenches, emphasizing the darker, more corroded surfaces and the symmetry of the open jaws.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the lighter wrench in the foreground and the open jaws of the other two wrenches, with less visible text.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Visible rust and wear marks on the darker wrenches, especially along the shafts and jaws; moderate severity.
